sentence_reviews.text: Markovian state, transition, action, observation, and belief representations formalize sequential uncertainty and control.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Sutton and Barto MDP formalism; AIMA MDP/POMDP and belief-state material; PML state-space/Markov modeling context.
sentence_reviews.notes: MDPs/POMDPs directly involve states, transitions, actions, observations, and beliefs for sequential decision making under uncertainty. The sentence combines multiple related formalisms.

sentence_reviews.text: The role of Markov chains, MDPs, POMDPs & belief states is to connect Probability theory, Sequences, events & time series to downstream capabilities including Reinforcement learning.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Sutton and Barto formulate RL with MDPs; AIMA and PML cover probabilistic sequential models.
sentence_reviews.notes: The MDP-to-RL connection is directly supported; the probability and sequence dependencies are synthesized from the formalism.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Sutton and Barto use MDPs as the standard formal framework for reinforcement learning; AIMA covers MDPs in decision making.
sentence_reviews.notes: MDPs are directly presented as a core formal basis for RL.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: MDP transition dynamics and Markov chains are probabilistic constructs in RL/PML treatments.
sentence_reviews.notes: Transition probabilities and belief states directly use probability theory.

sentence_reviews.text: Encoding choices can discard information, amplify bias, or make important invariances difficult to learn. Its direct dependencies include Probability theory, Sequences, events & time series.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: The sources cover MDP/POMDP assumptions but not this generic encoding limitation sentence.
sentence_reviews.notes: The generic limitation is not directly supported by packet evidence, though Markov assumptions have known limitations.

sentence_reviews.text: Markov chains, MDPs, POMDPs & belief states rests on the formal or computational capabilities supplied by Probability theory.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Transition probabilities in MDPs/Markov chains and belief states in POMDPs.
sentence_reviews.notes: The formalism directly uses probability distributions and conditional transitions.

sentence_reviews.text: Markov chains, MDPs, POMDPs & belief states consumes, emits, or organizes information using Sequences, events & time series.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Markov decision processes model time-indexed state/action/reward transitions; PML covers sequential/state-space models.
sentence_reviews.notes: Sequential organization is inherent, though the rationale wording is atlas-generated.

sentence_reviews.text: Markov chains, MDPs, POMDPs & belief states rests on the formal or computational capabilities supplied by Game theory & decision theory.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA decision-theoretic agent and MDP material; Sutton and Barto sequential decision-making formalism.
sentence_reviews.notes: Decision theory is strongly relevant to MDPs. Game theory is broader and not necessary for single-agent MDPs, making the combined dependency only partially supported.

sentence_reviews.text: Markov chains, MDPs, POMDPs & belief states rests on the formal or computational capabilities supplied by Dynamical systems & control theory.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Sequential control framing in RL and AIMA decision-making material.
sentence_reviews.notes: Control-theoretic framing is relevant for some MDP/POMDP uses, but Markov chains do not generally require dynamical systems/control theory.

edge_reviews.notes: Markov chains, MDPs, POMDPs, and belief states directly use probabilities.
The formalisms organize state/action/observation over time; represents_with/core is defensible.
Decision theory is supported; game theory is not generally required for MDPs. The combined dependency label weakens exact support.
Control is relevant to sequential decision processes, but not clearly a common prerequisite for all Markov chains/MDPs/POMDP belief states.
